task: Explore fastapi/routing.py and extract the APIRoute construction pattern as a reusable skill.
Map how fastapi/dependencies/utils.py resolves dependency callables and encode it as a verified skill.
Read fastapi/params.py and write a skill that builds Param subclasses following the same metaclass-free pattern.
Inspect fastapi/security/ and extract the common scheme __call__ pattern as a skill.
Walk fastapi/openapi/utils.py and identify how schemas get inlined; encode the inlining helper as a skill.
Read fastapi/exception_handlers.py and write a skill that constructs JSONResponse error payloads in the same shape.
Look at fastapi/encoders.py jsonable_encoder and produce a smaller reusable encoder skill following its dispatch style.
Trace fastapi/applications.py FastAPI.__init__ and list the most common middleware-related attribute names.
Read fastapi/middleware/__init__.py and produce a skill that re-exports starlette middleware with the fastapi naming convention.
Examine fastapi/responses.py and write a skill that builds a custom JSONResponse subclass following fastapi's style.
Inspect fastapi/datastructures.py and extract any utility classes that wrap starlette equivalents.
Read fastapi/_compat.py and identify how it switches between pydantic v1 and v2; encode the dispatch as a skill.
Look at fastapi/utils.py and extract any small pure helper as a skill.
Walk fastapi/routing.py APIRouter.add_api_route and document the parameter ordering as a skill.
Read fastapi/dependencies/models.py and produce a skill that constructs a Dependant object correctly.
Examine fastapi/openapi/models.py and identify the base model class used for OpenAPI schemas.
Read fastapi/security/oauth2.py and extract the OAuth2 flow construction pattern as a skill.
Look at fastapi/concurrency.py and encode the contextmanager-to-async helper as a reusable skill.
Inspect fastapi/cli.py and identify how it discovers the FastAPI app object; encode as a skill.
Read fastapi/applications.py and write a skill that mounts a sub-application following the same conventions.
List every module inside fastapi/ that imports from starlette and report which starlette names each one imports.
Find the file that defines the FastAPI class and report the name of its parent class plus the line number of the class definition.
Write a new APIRoute subclass that follows the constructor signature and attribute conventions used in fastapi/routing.py APIRoute, adding a single new attribute called custom_tag.

Write a new Depends-style helper named DependsCached that mirrors the structure of fastapi/param_functions.py Depends but caches its result by argument tuple. Match the existing param_functions.py style.
Find a code pattern that is repeated across at least three files in fastapi/ and write a verified Python skill that abstracts it. Cite the three source files.
Synthesize how fastapi handles request validation across params.py, dependencies/utils.py, and routing.py and produce a working minimal reproduction in under 60 lines.

Parse this pain.001 XML and extract all party fields. The message is: <Document xmlns="urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:pain.001.001.09"><CstmrCdtTrfInitn><GrpHdr><MsgId>MERIDIAN-20251119-001</MsgId><CreDtTm>2025-11-19T09:30:00Z</CreDtTm><NbOfTxs>1</NbOfTxs><CtrlSum>47832.50</CtrlSum></GrpHdr><PmtInf><PmtMtd>TRF</PmtMtd><ReqdExctnDt>2025-11-19</ReqdExctnDt><Dbtr><Nm>Meridian Ltd</Nm><PstlAdr><StrtNm>City Road</StrtNm><BldgNb>123</BldgNb><TwnNm>London</TwnNm><Ctry>GB</Ctry></PstlAdr></Dbtr><DbtrAcct><Id><IBAN>GB29NWBK60161331926819</IBAN></Id></DbtrAcct><CdtTrfTxInf><EndToEndId>E2E-INV-2025-0847</EndToEndId><Amt><InstdAmt Ccy="EUR">47832.50</InstdAmt></Amt><CdtrAgt><FinInstnId><BICFI>DEUTDEDB</BICFI></FinInstnId></CdtrAgt><Cdtr><Nm>Fischer GmbH</Nm><PstlAdr><StrtNm>Industriestrasse</StrtNm><BldgNb>45</BldgNb><TwnNm>Frankfurt am Main</TwnNm><Ctry>DE</Ctry></PstlAdr></Cdtr><CdtrAcct><Id><IBAN>DE89370400440532013000</IBAN></Id></CdtrAcct><RmtInf><Ustrd>Invoice INV-2025-0847 industrial components</Ustrd></RmtInf></CdtTrfTxInf></PmtInf></CstmrCdtTrfInitn></Document>. Write a Python function parse_pain001_fields(xml_string: str) -> dict that extracts: msg_id, e2e_id, debtor_name, debtor_iban, debtor_country, creditor_name, creditor_iban, creditor_bic, creditor_country, amount, currency, remittance_info. Include a unit test that parses the above XML and asserts all fields are present and correct.

Fetch the current EUR to GBP exchange rate from the ECB Frankfurt API at https://api.frankfurter.app/latest?from=EUR&to=GBP using the urllib.request library. Write a Python function fetch_eur_gbp_rate() -> dict that returns: rate (float), from_currency (str), to_currency (str), date (str), source (str). If the API is unavailable, fall back to a cached rate of 0.8530 and set source to cached. Include a unit test that calls the function and asserts the returned dict has all required keys and rate is a positive float. This skill has audit_required: true.

Construct a complete ISO 20022 pacs.008.001.08 XML message from the pain.001 fields extracted in task 1. Write a Python function build_pacs008(pain001_fields: dict, settlement_rate: float) -> str that produces a valid pacs.008 XML string. The pacs.008 must include: GrpHdr with MsgId (prefix SWIFT- to the original MsgId), NbOfTxs, SttlmInf with SttlmMtd CLSS. CdtTrfTxInf with PmtId containing EndToEndId and TxId, IntrBkSttlmAmt in EUR, IntrBkSttlmDt, ChrgBr SHA, DbtrAgt BIC NWBKGB2L, Dbtr with name and structured address (TwnNm and Ctry minimum), DbtrAcct IBAN, CdtrAgt BIC from pain001_fields, Cdtr with name and fully structured address, CdtrAcct IBAN, RmtInf Ustrd. Include a unit test that builds a pacs.008 from the Meridian-Fischer fields and asserts the XML is parseable and contains EndToEndId E2E-INV-2025-0847.

Build a pacs.002.001.10 payment status report accepting the transaction. Write a Python function build_pacs002_acceptance(original_msg_id: str, original_e2e_id: str, original_tx_id: str, settlement_amount: float, settlement_currency: str, settlement_date: str) -> str that produces a valid pacs.002 XML string. The status report must include: GrpHdr with new MsgId (prefix ACK- to original), TxInfAndSts with OrgnlEndToEndId, OrgnlTxId, TxSts of ACCP, AccptncDtTm as current UTC time, SttlmInf with SttlmDt and SttlmAmt. Include a unit test that builds the acceptance for the Meridian-Fischer transaction and asserts TxSts is ACCP and OrgnlEndToEndId matches.

Construct a camt.054.001.08 bank to customer debit credit notification for Fischer GmbH. Write a Python function build_camt054_notification(creditor_iban: str, creditor_name: str, amount: float, currency: str, booking_date: str, e2e_id: str, debtor_name: str, remittance_info: str) -> str that produces a valid camt.054 XML string. The notification must include: GrpHdr with MsgId (prefix NTFN-), Ntfctn with account IBAN, Ntry with Amt, CdtDbtInd CRDT, Sts Cd BOOK, BookgDt, ValDt, NtryDtls TxDtls containing Refs EndToEndId, RltdPties Dbtr name, RmtInf Ustrd. Include a unit test that builds the notification for Fischer GmbH receiving EUR 47832.50 and asserts CdtDbtInd is CRDT and the IBAN appears in the output.

Reconcile the following payment message chain and produce a structured report. Write a Python function reconcile_payment_chain(messages: list) -> dict where each message has type (pain001/pacs008/pacs002/camt054), msg_id, e2e_id, amount, currency, and status fields. The function must: verify all messages share the same e2e_id, verify amounts are consistent across messages, count matched vs unmatched, compute a discrepancy amount, return: e2e_id (str), total_messages (int), matched (int), unmatched (int), amount_discrepancy (float), currency (str), chain_valid (bool), verdict (str), invoice_status (str set to PAID if chain_valid). Include a unit test with a valid 4-message chain for E2E-INV-2025-0847 asserting chain_valid is True and invoice_status is PAID.

Parse this pain.001 XML and extract all party fields. Write a Python function parse_pain001_fields(xml_string: str) -> dict that uses xml.etree.ElementTree to extract: msg_id, e2e_id, debtor_name, debtor_iban, debtor_country, creditor_name, creditor_iban, creditor_bic, creditor_country, amount, currency, remittance_info. Handle the namespace urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:pain.001.001.09. The function must use only stdlib. Include a unit test inside the PY SKILL block: if __name__ == "__main__": xml = '<Document xmlns="urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:pain.001.001.09"><CstmrCdtTrfInitn><GrpHdr><MsgId>MERIDIAN-20251119-001</MsgId></GrpHdr><PmtInf><Dbtr><Nm>Meridian Ltd</Nm><PstlAdr><Ctry>GB</Ctry></PstlAdr></Dbtr><DbtrAcct><Id><IBAN>GB29NWBK60161331926819</IBAN></Id></DbtrAcct><CdtTrfTxInf><EndToEndId>E2E-INV-2025-0847</EndToEndId><Amt><InstdAmt Ccy="EUR">47832.50</InstdAmt></Amt><CdtrAgt><FinInstnId><BICFI>DEUTDEDB</BICFI></FinInstnId></CdtrAgt><Cdtr><Nm>Fischer GmbH</Nm><PstlAdr><Ctry>DE</Ctry></PstlAdr></Cdtr><CdtrAcct><Id><IBAN>DE89370400440532013000</IBAN></Id></CdtrAcct><RmtInf><Ustrd>Invoice INV-2025-0847</Ustrd></RmtInf></CdtTrfTxInf></PmtInf></CstmrCdtTrfInitn></Document>'; result = parse_pain001_fields(xml); assert isinstance(result, dict); assert result.get("msg_id") == "MERIDIAN-20251119-001"; assert result.get("creditor_bic") == "DEUTDEDB"; assert result.get("amount") == "47832.50" or result.get("amount") == 47832.50; print("TEST PASSED")

Validate IBANs using the MOD-97 checksum algorithm. Write a Python function validate_iban(iban: str) -> dict that returns a dict with keys: valid (bool), country (str), check_digits (str), bank_code (str), reason (str). Algorithm: remove spaces, move first 4 chars to end, replace each letter with its numeric value (A=10 through Z=35), compute int mod 97, result must equal 1 for valid. Use only Python stdlib. Include a unit test inside the PY SKILL block: if __name__ == "__main__": r1 = validate_iban("DE89370400440532013000"); assert r1["valid"] == True, f"DE IBAN should be valid, got {r1}"; assert r1["country"] == "DE"; r2 = validate_iban("GB29NWBK60161331926819"); assert r2["valid"] == True, f"GB IBAN should be valid, got {r2}"; r3 = validate_iban("DE00000000000000000000"); assert r3["valid"] == False, "Invalid IBAN should fail"; print("TEST PASSED")

Screen parties against sanctions using pattern matching. Write a Python function screen_parties(parties: list) -> dict where each party dict has "name" (str) and "country" (str) keys. Check each party against HIGH_RISK_COUNTRIES = ["IR","KP","SY","CU","RU","BY"] and SANCTIONED_FRAGMENTS = ["al-qaeda","isis","daesh","hamas","hezbollah"]. A party matches if its country is in HIGH_RISK_COUNTRIES or its name lowercased contains any fragment. Return: cleared (bool, True if no matches), parties_checked (int), matches (list of matching party names), risk_score (int, 25 * len(matches) capped at 100), decision (str, "APPROVE" if cleared else "BLOCK"). Use only Python stdlib. Include a unit test inside the PY SKILL block: if __name__ == "__main__": parties = [{"name":"Meridian Ltd","country":"GB"},{"name":"Fischer GmbH","country":"DE"},{"name":"NatWest Bank plc","country":"GB"},{"name":"Deutsche Bank AG","country":"DE"}]; result = screen_parties(parties); assert result["cleared"] == True, f"Should clear, got {result}"; assert result["parties_checked"] == 4; assert result["decision"] == "APPROVE"; r2 = screen_parties([{"name":"hamas charity","country":"GB"}]); assert r2["cleared"] == False; print("TEST PASSED")

Fetch the EUR to GBP exchange rate. Write a Python function fetch_eur_gbp_rate() -> dict that returns: rate (float), from_currency (str), to_currency (str), date (str), source (str). Try urllib.request.urlopen("https://api.frankfurter.app/latest?from=EUR&to=GBP", timeout=5), parse the JSON response, extract rate from response["rates"]["GBP"]. If any exception occurs, return rate=0.8530, source="cached" with from_currency="EUR", to_currency="GBP", date="cached". Use only Python stdlib (urllib.request, json). Include a unit test inside the PY SKILL block: if __name__ == "__main__": result = fetch_eur_gbp_rate(); assert isinstance(result, dict), "must return dict"; assert "rate" in result and isinstance(result["rate"], float), f"rate must be float, got {result}"; assert result["rate"] > 0, "rate must be positive"; assert result["from_currency"] == "EUR"; assert result["to_currency"] == "GBP"; assert "source" in result; print("TEST PASSED")

Build a pacs.008.001.08 XML message. Write a Python function build_pacs008(pain001_fields: dict, settlement_rate: float) -> str that constructs a pacs.008 XML string using xml.etree.ElementTree. The XML must have namespace urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:pacs.008.001.08 and contain: GrpHdr/MsgId = "SWIFT-" + pain001_fields.get("msg_id",""), GrpHdr/NbOfTxs = "1", SttlmInf/SttlmMtd = "CLSS", CdtTrfTxInf/PmtId/EndToEndId = pain001_fields.get("e2e_id",""), CdtTrfTxInf/IntrBkSttlmAmt with Ccy attribute = pain001_fields.get("currency","EUR") and text = str(pain001_fields.get("amount","")), ChrgBr = "SHA", DbtrAgt BIC = "NWBKGB2L", Cdtr/Nm = pain001_fields.get("creditor_name",""), CdtrAcct IBAN = pain001_fields.get("creditor_iban",""). Use xml.etree.ElementTree and return ET.tostring(..., encoding="unicode"). Use only stdlib. Include a unit test inside the PY SKILL block: if __name__ == "__main__": import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; fields = {"msg_id":"MERIDIAN-001","e2e_id":"E2E-INV-2025-0847","creditor_name":"Fischer GmbH","creditor_iban":"DE89370400440532013000","creditor_bic":"DEUTDEDB","currency":"EUR","amount":"47832.50"}; xml_str = build_pacs008(fields, 0.853); assert isinstance(xml_str, str) and len(xml_str) > 50, "must return non-empty string"; assert "E2E-INV-2025-0847" in xml_str, f"EndToEndId missing: {xml_str[:200]}"; assert "SWIFT-MERIDIAN-001" in xml_str or "MERIDIAN" in xml_str; print("TEST PASSED")

Build a pacs.002.001.10 payment status report. Write a Python function build_pacs002_acceptance(original_msg_id: str, original_e2e_id: str, original_tx_id: str, settlement_amount: float, settlement_currency: str, settlement_date: str) -> str that constructs a pacs.002 XML string using xml.etree.ElementTree. The XML must contain: GrpHdr/MsgId = "ACK-" + original_msg_id, TxInfAndSts/OrgnlEndToEndId = original_e2e_id, TxInfAndSts/TxSts = "ACCP", SttlmInf/SttlmDt = settlement_date, SttlmInf/SttlmAmt = str(settlement_amount). Use only stdlib. Include a unit test inside the PY SKILL block: if __name__ == "__main__": result = build_pacs002_acceptance("MERIDIAN-001","E2E-INV-2025-0847","TXN-001",47832.50,"EUR","2025-11-19"); assert isinstance(result, str) and len(result) > 50, "must return non-empty XML string"; assert "ACCP" in result, f"TxSts ACCP missing: {result[:200]}"; assert "E2E-INV-2025-0847" in result, "OrgnlEndToEndId missing"; assert "ACK-MERIDIAN-001" in result, "ACK prefix missing"; print("TEST PASSED")

Build a camt.054.001.08 debit credit notification. Write a Python function build_camt054_notification(creditor_iban: str, creditor_name: str, amount: float, currency: str, booking_date: str, e2e_id: str, debtor_name: str, remittance_info: str) -> str that constructs a camt.054 XML string using xml.etree.ElementTree. The XML must contain: GrpHdr/MsgId starting with "NTFN-", Ntfctn with IBAN = creditor_iban, Ntry/Amt = str(amount) with Ccy attribute = currency, CdtDbtInd = "CRDT", Sts/Cd = "BOOK", BookgDt = booking_date, EndToEndId = e2e_id, Dbtr/Nm = debtor_name, RmtInf/Ustrd = remittance_info. Use only stdlib. Include a unit test inside the PY SKILL block: if __name__ == "__main__": result = build_camt054_notification("DE89370400440532013000","Fischer GmbH",47832.50,"EUR","2025-11-19","E2E-INV-2025-0847","Meridian Ltd","Invoice INV-2025-0847"); assert isinstance(result, str) and len(result) > 50, "must return XML string"; assert "CRDT" in result, "CdtDbtInd CRDT missing"; assert "DE89370400440532013000" in result, "IBAN missing"; assert "NTFN-" in result, "NTFN prefix missing"; print("TEST PASSED")

Reconcile a payment message chain. Write a Python function reconcile_payment_chain(messages: list) -> dict where each message is a dict with keys: type (str), msg_id (str), e2e_id (str), amount (float), currency (str), status (str). The function must: check all messages share the same e2e_id (first message's e2e_id is reference), check all amounts equal the first message amount, count matched (same e2e_id and amount) vs unmatched, compute amount_discrepancy as sum of abs differences from reference amount. Return: e2e_id (str), total_messages (int), matched (int), unmatched (int), amount_discrepancy (float), currency (str), chain_valid (bool, True if unmatched==0 and amount_discrepancy==0), verdict (str), invoice_status (str, "PAID" if chain_valid else "UNPAID"). Use only stdlib. Include a unit test inside the PY SKILL block: if __name__ == "__main__": chain = [{"type":"pain001","msg_id":"M1","e2e_id":"E2E-INV-2025-0847","amount":47832.50,"currency":"EUR","status":"INIT"},{"type":"pacs008","msg_id":"M2","e2e_id":"E2E-INV-2025-0847","amount":47832.50,"currency":"EUR","status":"SENT"},{"type":"pacs002","msg_id":"M3","e2e_id":"E2E-INV-2025-0847","amount":47832.50,"currency":"EUR","status":"ACCP"},{"type":"camt054","msg_id":"M4","e2e_id":"E2E-INV-2025-0847","amount":47832.50,"currency":"EUR","status":"BOOK"}]; result = reconcile_payment_chain(chain); assert result["chain_valid"] == True, f"chain should be valid: {result}"; assert result["invoice_status"] == "PAID", f"invoice_status should be PAID: {result}"; assert result["matched"] == 4; assert result["unmatched"] == 0; print("TEST PASSED")

Parse this pain.001 XML and extract all party fields. Write a Python function parse_pain001_fields(xml_string: str) -> dict that uses xml.etree.ElementTree to extract: msg_id, e2e_id, debtor_name, debtor_iban, debtor_country, creditor_name, creditor_iban, creditor_bic, creditor_country, amount, currency, remittance_info. Handle the namespace urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:pain.001.001.09. The function must use only stdlib. Include a unit test inside the PY SKILL block: if __name__ == "__main__": xml = '<Document xmlns="urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:pain.001.001.09"><CstmrCdtTrfInitn><GrpHdr><MsgId>MERIDIAN-20251119-001</MsgId></GrpHdr><PmtInf><Dbtr><Nm>Meridian Ltd</Nm><PstlAdr><Ctry>GB</Ctry></PstlAdr></Dbtr><DbtrAcct><Id><IBAN>GB29NWBK60161331926819</IBAN></Id></DbtrAcct><CdtTrfTxInf><EndToEndId>E2E-INV-2025-0847</EndToEndId><Amt><InstdAmt Ccy="EUR">47832.50</InstdAmt></Amt><CdtrAgt><FinInstnId><BICFI>DEUTDEDB</BICFI></FinInstnId></CdtrAgt><Cdtr><Nm>Fischer GmbH</Nm><PstlAdr><Ctry>DE</Ctry></PstlAdr></Cdtr><CdtrAcct><Id><IBAN>DE89370400440532013000</IBAN></Id></CdtrAcct><RmtInf><Ustrd>Invoice INV-2025-0847</Ustrd></RmtInf></CdtTrfTxInf></PmtInf></CstmrCdtTrfInitn></Document>'; result = parse_pain001_fields(xml); assert isinstance(result, dict); assert result.get("msg_id") == "MERIDIAN-20251119-001"; assert result.get("creditor_bic") == "DEUTDEDB"; assert result.get("amount") == "47832.50" or result.get("amount") == 47832.50; print("TEST PASSED")

Screen parties against sanctions using pattern matching. Write a Python function screen_parties(parties: list) -> dict where each party dict has "name" (str) and "country" (str) keys. Check each party against HIGH_RISK_COUNTRIES = ["IR","KP","SY","CU","RU","BY"] and SANCTIONED_FRAGMENTS = ["al-qaeda","isis","daesh","hamas","hezbollah"]. A party matches if its country is in HIGH_RISK_COUNTRIES or its name lowercased contains any fragment. Return: cleared (bool, True if no matches), parties_checked (int), matches (list of matching party names), risk_score (int, 25 * len(matches) capped at 100), decision (str, "APPROVE" if cleared else "BLOCK"). Use only Python stdlib. Include a unit test inside the PY SKILL block: if __name__ == "__main__": parties = [{"name":"Meridian Ltd","country":"GB"},{"name":"Fischer GmbH","country":"DE"},{"name":"NatWest Bank plc","country":"GB"},{"name":"Deutsche Bank AG","country":"DE"}]; result = screen_parties(parties); assert result["cleared"] == True, f"Should clear, got {result}"; assert result["parties_checked"] == 4; assert result["decision"] == "APPROVE"; r2 = screen_parties([{"name":"hamas charity","country":"GB"}]); assert r2["cleared"] == False; print("TEST PASSED")

Fetch the EUR to GBP exchange rate. Write a Python function fetch_eur_gbp_rate() -> dict that returns: rate (float), from_currency (str), to_currency (str), date (str), source (str). Try urllib.request.urlopen("https://api.frankfurter.app/latest?from=EUR&to=GBP", timeout=5), parse the JSON response, extract rate from response["rates"]["GBP"]. If any exception occurs, return rate=0.8530, source="cached" with from_currency="EUR", to_currency="GBP", date="cached". Use only Python stdlib (urllib.request, json). Include a unit test inside the PY SKILL block: if __name__ == "__main__": result = fetch_eur_gbp_rate(); assert isinstance(result, dict), "must return dict"; assert "rate" in result and isinstance(result["rate"], float), f"rate must be float, got {result}"; assert result["rate"] > 0, "rate must be positive"; assert result["from_currency"] == "EUR"; assert result["to_currency"] == "GBP"; assert "source" in result; print("TEST PASSED")
